meta.title: Is iCloud's Free Tier Still 5GB?
meta.description: Yes. In this day and age, Apple still gives you a whopping 5GB of free iCloud storage.
meta.og_description: Yes. Apple still gives you only 5GB free.
meta.twitter_description: Yes. Apple still gives you a whopping 5GB of free iCloud storage. Unbelievable.

info.body_html: Apple, a $3+ trillion company, still offers the same 5GB of free iCloud storage it launched with in 2011. Google gives you 15GB.

share.heading: Send this to your group chat:
share.subheading: (or your mom who always keeps running out of storage)
share.threads_share_text: Is iCloud's free tier still 5GB? Yes. https://isicloud5gb.lol

news.heading: Subscribe to news.
news.body: Stay in touch for when Apple finally updates their free tier.

footer.disclaimer: Not affiliated with Apple. Just disappointed.
footer.maintained_by_html: Maintained by Yury Vashchylau — Twitter, GitHub, LinkedIn

readme.title: Is iCloud's Free tier still 5GB?

faq.q: Has it changed since the last time I checked?

faq.a: That's not a question, but yes, it does. Tripling Apple's offering. For free.
Check back at [isicloud5gb.lol](https://isicloud5gb.lol). If the site is still up, the answer is still no.

readme.tech_stack: This entire website has more meta tags than content, because the answer is one word. Feel free to overengineer it as much as you want.
readme.contributing_intro: There's not much to contribute. The answer is *yes*.
readme.contributing_body: To help us on our mission to deliver this answer, the wishlist is:
readme.translations: Help translate this site into your language via [Weblate](https://hosted.weblate.org/engage/isicloud5gb/):
spread_items: share this website on your socials
send it to your family's group chat
ask grandma if she ran out of iCloud storage today and keeps deleting those family archive pics that have never been backed up
readme.shutdown_notice: But if the Apple ever changes the answer to this question, open a PR and we'll shut the whole site down.
readme.license_joke: Because unlike iCloud storage, this is actually *free*.
